name: Example pack — GraphQL Federation
description: Reference content pack showing the exact JSON structure. Copy this file as a starting point for your own packs.

topics.name: GraphQL Federation
topics.description: Composing one GraphQL API from multiple subgraph services.

questions.title: GraphQL Federation basics
questions.prompt: What problem does GraphQL Federation solve, and when would you use it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Definition, the single-graph-many-teams problem, subgraphs and the router, and one trade-off.

expectedPoints.label: Defines federation
expectedPoints.description: Explains that multiple services own parts of one composed GraphQL schema.

expectedPoints.label: Mentions team/domain ownership
expectedPoints.description: Mentions that teams can own and deploy their part of the graph independently.

expectedPoints.label: Names a trade-off
expectedPoints.description: Mentions router as a critical dependency, added complexity, or query planning cost.

followUps.prompt: When would GraphQL Federation be overkill?

questions.title: Federation vs single GraphQL server
questions.prompt: Your company has one GraphQL server maintained by four teams and deploys are painful. Would you move to federation?

expectedPoints.label: Anchors in requirements
expectedPoints.description: Asks about team pain points, scale, and whether modularization within one server would be enough.

expectedPoints.label: Considers migration and operational cost
expectedPoints.description: Mentions router infrastructure, schema composition checks, and the migration path.

followUps.prompt: Suppose you do migrate — which part of the schema would you extract as the first subgraph, and why?
